From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Head of state and of government of the U.S. state of New Hampshire

For a list, see [List of governors of New Hampshire](/wiki/List_of_governors_of_New_Hampshire "List of governors of New Hampshire").

| Governor of New Hampshire | |
| --- | --- |
| [State seal](/wiki/Seal_of_New_Hampshire "Seal of New Hampshire") | |
| Incumbent [Kelly Ayotte](/wiki/Kelly_Ayotte "Kelly Ayotte") since January 9, 2025 | |
| [Government of New Hampshire](/wiki/Government_of_New_Hampshire "Government of New Hampshire") | |
| [Style](/wiki/Style_(form_of_address) "Style (form of address)") | * [Governor](/wiki/Governor "Governor") (informal) * [Her Excellency](/wiki/Excellency#United_States "Excellency")[[1]](#cite_note-1) (formal) |
| Status | * [Head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") * [Head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government") |
| Member of | [Governor's Council](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_New_Hampshire "Executive Council of New Hampshire") [Cabinet](/wiki/Government_of_New_Hampshire#Executive "Government of New Hampshire") |
| [Residence](/wiki/Official_residence "Official residence") | [Bridges House](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Governor%27s_Mansion "New Hampshire Governor's Mansion") |
| [Seat](/wiki/Seat_of_government "Seat of government") | [Concord](/wiki/Concord,_New_Hampshire "Concord, New Hampshire"), [New Hampshire](/wiki/New_Hampshire "New Hampshire") |
| [Term length](/wiki/Term_of_office "Term of office") | Two years, no term limits |
| Constituting instrument | [New Hampshire Constitution of 1776](/wiki/Constitution_of_New_Hampshire#1776_Constitution "Constitution of New Hampshire") |
| Precursor | * [Governor of Strawbery Banke](/wiki/List_of_colonial_governors_of_New_Hampshire#Lower_plantation_governors,_1630–1641 "List of colonial governors of New Hampshire") 1630–1641 * [Governor of Dover](/wiki/List_of_colonial_governors_of_New_Hampshire#Upper_plantation_governors,_1631–1641 "List of colonial governors of New Hampshire") 1631–1641 * [Governor of Massachusetts Bay](/wiki/List_of_colonial_governors_of_New_Hampshire#Massachusetts_governors,_1641–1680 "List of colonial governors of New Hampshire") 1641–1680 |
| Formation | January 21, 1680 (346 years ago) (1680-01-21) |
| Succession | [Line of succession](/wiki/Gubernatorial_lines_of_succession_in_the_United_States#New_Hampshire "Gubernatorial lines of succession in the United States") |
| Salary | $146,172 [[2]](#cite_note-2) |
| Website | [Official website](http://governor.nh.gov) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q5589687#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") |

The **governor of New Hampshire** is the [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government") of the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New Hampshire](/wiki/New_Hampshire "New Hampshire").

The governor is elected during the biennial state general election in November of even-numbered years. [New Hampshire](/wiki/New_Hampshire "New Hampshire") is one of only two states, along with bordering [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont"), to hold gubernatorial elections every two years as opposed to every four. Currently, the state's 83rd governor is [Republican](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)") [Kelly Ayotte](/wiki/Kelly_Ayotte "Kelly Ayotte"), who has served since January 9, 2025.

In New Hampshire, the governor has no [term limit](/wiki/Term_limit "Term limit") of any kind. Only two governors have served more than three terms since the 18th century (when the term was for only one year), [John Lynch](/wiki/John_Lynch_(New_Hampshire_governor) "John Lynch (New Hampshire governor)"), who won a fourth two-year term on November 2, 2010, and [Chris Sununu](/wiki/Chris_Sununu "Chris Sununu"), who won a fourth two-year term on November 8, 2022.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [John Taylor Gilman](/wiki/John_Taylor_Gilman "John Taylor Gilman") had been the last governor before Lynch to serve longer than six years, serving 14 one-year terms as governor between 1794 and 1816. Gilman is one of seven governors to serve non-consecutive terms, the others being [John Langdon](/wiki/John_Langdon_(politician) "John Langdon (politician)"), [John Sullivan](/wiki/John_Sullivan_(general) "John Sullivan (general)"), [William Plumer](/wiki/William_Plumer "William Plumer"), [Benjamin Pierce](/wiki/Benjamin_Pierce_(governor) "Benjamin Pierce (governor)"), [James A. Weston](/wiki/James_A._Weston "James A. Weston"), and [John Gilbert Winant](/wiki/John_Gilbert_Winant "John Gilbert Winant").

Unlike in many other states in which executive councils are merely advisory, the [Executive Council of New Hampshire](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_New_Hampshire "Executive Council of New Hampshire") has a strong check on the governor's power. The five-member council has a veto over many actions of the governor. Together, the governor and Executive Council approve contracts with a value of $5,000 or more, approve [pardons](/wiki/Pardon "Pardon"), and appoint the directors and commissioners, judges, the [attorney general](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Attorney_General "New Hampshire Attorney General") and officers in the [National Guard](/wiki/New_Hampshire_National_Guard "New Hampshire National Guard").

To be qualified to be governor, one must be 30 years of age or older, a registered voter, and domiciled in New Hampshire for at least seven years.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Title

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governor_of_New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Title")]

Traditionally, the [governors](/wiki/List_of_colonial_governors_of_New_Hampshire "List of colonial governors of New Hampshire") of the colonial [Province of New Hampshire](/wiki/Province_of_New_Hampshire "Province of New Hampshire") were titled as "President of New Hampshire", beginning with the appointment of the province's first president, [John Cutt](/wiki/John_Cutt "John Cutt"), in 1679. After independence, from 1786 to 1791, "President of the State of New Hampshire" was the official style of the position. The [New Hampshire Constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_New_Hampshire "Constitution of New Hampshire") was amended in 1791 to replace "President" with "Governor".

## Function

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governor_of_New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Function")]

The [Constitution of New Hampshire](/wiki/Constitution_of_New_Hampshire "Constitution of New Hampshire") details the duties and powers of the governor:[[5]](#cite_note-5)

* Act as a supreme executive magistrate
* Shall be responsible for the faithful execution of the laws
* When a disagreement exists between the two legislative chambers, the governor with advice of the executive council shall have the right to adjourn or prorogue the general court
* The governor may direct a session of the General Court to be held at another location within the state in cases where there is danger to the health or lives of the members
* Power to [veto](/wiki/Veto "Veto") bills and resolutions presented to the governor by the General Court
* Nomination of all judicial officers, the attorney general, and all officers of the militia with the approval of the executive council
* Act as the [commander-in-chief](/wiki/Commander-in-chief "Commander-in-chief") for all military forces of the state
* Power to [pardon](/wiki/Pardon "Pardon") offenses, except those convicted by the Senate and impeached by the House
* Money issued out of the state treasury shall be by warrant under the hand of the governor and with advice of the council

## Succession

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governor_of_New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Succession")]

Main article: [Gubernatorial lines of succession in the United States § New Hampshire](/wiki/Gubernatorial_lines_of_succession_in_the_United_States#New_Hampshire "Gubernatorial lines of succession in the United States")

Established by Part 2, Article 49 of the [Constitution of New Hampshire](/wiki/Constitution_of_New_Hampshire "Constitution of New Hampshire").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

| # | Office | Current officer |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | [President](/wiki/List_of_presidents_of_the_New_Hampshire_Senate "List of presidents of the New Hampshire Senate") of the [Senate](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Senate "New Hampshire Senate") | [Sharon Carson](/wiki/Sharon_Carson "Sharon Carson") (R) |
| 2 | Speaker of the [House of Representatives](/wiki/New_Hampshire_House_of_Representatives "New Hampshire House of Representatives") | [Sherman Packard](/wiki/Sherman_Packard "Sherman Packard") (R) |
| 3 | [Secretary of State](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Secretary_of_State "New Hampshire Secretary of State") | [David Scanlan](/wiki/David_Scanlan "David Scanlan") (R) |
| 4 | State Treasurer | Monica Mezzapelle (D) |
